title: DCDC Kidney Care opens new dialysis facility in Faridabad
summary: DCDC Kidney Care has announced the opening of its independent dialysis facility in Faridabad. The facility intends to meet the increasing need for superior dialysis services in the area. With […]
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
15-machine centre aims to address rising demand for renal care services

DCDC Kidney Care has announced the opening of its independent dialysis facility in Faridabad. The facility intends to meet the increasing need for superior dialysis services in the area.

With 15 dialysis machines, the recently opened facility will provide services such as nephrologist consultations and maintenance hemodialysis. 

The facility to help about 120 patients yearly.

The center has applied for empanelment under the Ayushman Bharat Scheme in keeping with its goal of lowering the cost of dialysis services. The government-backed healthcare program will allow eligible patients to receive dialysis services upon approval, thereby lowering the cost of long-term care.

Aseem Garg, Founder and CEO, DCDC Kidney Care, said, “One of the biggest holes in India’s healthcare system is still access to high-quality dialysis, especially in rapidly expanding metropolitan areas like Faridabad. We are increasing continuity of care for patients who need lifelong therapy with this stand-alone facility, in addition to adding infrastructure. Our goal is to provide high-quality, standardised dialysis that is supported by clinical guidelines. We also strive for affordability through government initiatives like Ayushman Bharat. Our overarching goal of creating a strong, patient-centered network of renal care across the nation is reflected in this facility.”

Dr Deepak Agarwal, Vice President, Clinical Excellence and Academics, DCDC Kidney Care, said, “The results of dialysis are closely related to standards for infection control, clinical supervision, and consistency. To guarantee safe and efficient treatment, this facility has incorporated quality monitoring systems, regular nephrologist supervision, and standardized protocols. Access to prompt nephrology consultations will aid in better disease management and better patient outcomes outside of dialysis. Our goal is to transition from therapy to comprehensive renal care with a focus on clinical quality.”
